summary: The pre-commit type checks failed: mypy (and pyright) reported many type errors, primarily missing attributes and untyped-call errors related to the pyarrow API used in pandas.
root_cause: Mypy type checking failed because the pyarrow module (as seen by the type checker) lacks many attributes used by pandas (producing numerous 'Module has no attribute' [attr-defined] errors) and there are calls to untyped functions (e.g. 'fill_null') in typed contexts.

evidence.reason: Shows mypy type checking step failed.

evidence.quote: pandas/compat/pyarrow.py:69: error: Call to untyped function "fill_null" in typed context  [no-untyped-call]
evidence.reason: Concrete mypy error indicating calls to an untyped pyarrow function in a typed context.

evidence.quote: pandas/compat/pyarrow.py:76: error: Module has no attribute "is_null"  [attr-defined]
evidence.reason: Example of the repeated 'Module has no attribute' errors for pyarrow API members.

evidence.quote: pandas/core/arrays/arrow/array.py:101: error: Module has no attribute "equal"  [attr-defined]
evidence.reason: Shows attr-defined errors are widespread across arrow-related modules in the codebase.

evidence.quote: error: not checking stubs due to mypy build errors:
evidence.reason: Confirms that subsequent mypy stub checks were skipped because the build/type-check errors prevented progress.

suggested_fix: Ensure the type checker sees correct pyarrow types: install or provide type stubs for pyarrow (or upgrade to a pyarrow version that ships stubs), or add .pyi stubs for the missing attributes used by pandas. Alternatively, temporarily narrow typing (e.g., cast pyarrow objects to Any or mark untyped imports) in pandas/compat/pyarrow.py and related modules to silence no-untyped-call/attr-defined errors until proper stubs are available.
